bull_thesis: Liberty Global is a deep holdco-discount sum-of-parts unlock with a board-approved, sponsor-credible catalyst. On Feb 18, 2026 the board announced the creation of Ziggo Group (VodafoneZiggo + Telenet, ~13m customers, ~EUR6.6bn revenue) to be listed on Euronext Amsterdam in 2027 with 90% of shares spun to LBTY shareholders. Management is executing the identical playbook it ran successfully with the Sunrise spin-off completed in late 2024 — it has done exactly this, recently, and closed it. The enabling step, buying Vodafone's 50% of the VodafoneZiggo JV for EUR1.0bn cash + a 10% Ziggo stake, is expected to close H2 2026. The stock trades at ~0.41x book and ~$4bn market cap on businesses targeting ~EUR500m adjusted FCF by 2028 and deleveraging to ~4.5x — i.e. the market is pricing a punitive conglomerate discount that a clean, separately-listed, index-eligible Ziggo plus a simplified residual LBTY is designed to collapse. Aggressive ongoing buybacks shrink the share count every quarter, compounding per-share SoP. As the Vodafone close lands and the 2027 listing gets a hard date, the discount should narrow toward NAV.
bear_thesis: The spin is a 2027 window, not a fixed record/effective date — the ~378-day horizon is far and the listing year was hedged by management ('as we get into the second half of next year we'll be able to forecast the storyline'). The thesis depends on a regulatory condition precedent: the Vodafone JV buy-in (H2 2026) could draw an EU second-request or antitrust drag and slip the whole timeline. The underlying assets are levered European cable/telco facing fiber overbuild and mobile substitution — a secular multiple compressor that caps how much the spun entity re-rates, so even a clean separation may not close the full gap to book. If the discount simply persists (Europe holdco names chronically trade cheap) and the catalyst slips, the stock keeps trading as an ignored, levered cable holdco near its 52-week low (~$9.73). Leverage above the 4.5x 2028 target leaves refi/rate sensitivity in the interim.
sop_bull: Event-win value ~$18.00 (conservative re-rate as the spin de-risks). Derivation: a separately-listed Ziggo Group (~EUR6.6bn revenue, ~13m subs) valued on a regional-cable EV/EBITDA peer multiple, plus the residual LBTY (Telenet residual, VMO2 JV stake, Liberty Growth/ventures portfolio, Sunrise stub already separated, net liquidity), summed and discount-narrowed. Street/management base-case SoP sits nearer $27 and book value is ~$28+ (P/B 0.41), so $18 is a deliberate haircut-to-the-haircut, leaving upside headroom if the discount narrows even partway toward NAV. Buyback float-shrink compounds the per-share figure over the hold.
sop_bear: Verified downside floor ~$9.50. Derivation: standalone equity value of the levered operating assets if no re-rate occurs and the holdco discount persists — roughly the 52-week low ($9.73) and a 'deal slips / discount stays' world. Backed by real cash flows (~EUR500m adjusted FCF target by 2028) and a continuous buyback that supports per-share value even absent the spin. No going-concern question; this is leverage + discount, not insolvency, so the floor is real and well above zero.
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case. Verified target $18.00 (event resolves: Ziggo listed/spun on Euronext Amsterdam 2027, discount narrows; itself conservative vs ~$27 street/management SoP and ~$28+ book). Verified floor $9.50 (deal slips / discount persists, near 52-wk low). Weighting ~60% target / 40% floor given a board-approved, precedent-backed event (Sunrise 2024) but a soft 2027 date and an H2-2026 regulatory gate still pending: 0.6*18.00 + 0.4*9.50 = $14.60, rounded to ~$14.50. Skewed conservative for the far horizon.
risk_reward: Upside to target +$5.99 (~50%) vs downside to floor -$2.51 (~21%) from ~$12.01; R:R ~2.39. Asymmetry is wider against the NAV-true target (~$27 street SoP / ~$28 book) where upside exceeds 100%.
catalyst_summary: Board-approved Ziggo Group (VodafoneZiggo + Telenet) Euronext Amsterdam listing in 2027 with 90% spun to shareholders; gated on the Vodafone JV buy-in closing H2 2026 — ~50% upside to an $18 target vs a real ~$9.50 floor (R:R 2.39), Sunrise-2024 precedent de-risks execution.
dated_milestone: Vodafone VodafoneZiggo JV buy-in (EUR1.0bn cash + 10% Ziggo stake) expected to close H2 2026, subject to regulatory approval; Ziggo Group Euronext Amsterdam listing + 90% spin-off targeted for 2027 (board 8-K Feb 18, 2026).
forcing_function: H2-2026 regulatory close of the Vodafone JV acquisition (first hard gate), then the 2027 Euronext Amsterdam listing/record date for the Ziggo Group spin + index inclusion flow — the corporate event that forces the holdco discount to collapse.
consensus_delta: The street still anchors LBTYK to its trailing holdco discount and levered-European-cable optics (P/B 0.41, ~$4bn cap), treating the spin as perpetually-deferred Liberty financial engineering. The mispricing: the board has named the structure (Ziggo Group), the venue (Euronext Amsterdam), the year (2027), and the mechanism (90% distribution), and has the Sunrise 2024 precedent proving it closes these — yet the price embeds almost none of the unlock. Even a conservative $18 re-rate is a haircut to ~$27 street SoP and ~$28 book; consensus is anchored on 'discount forever' against a sponsor with a live, dated execution path.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 mo): the Vodafone JV buy-in must clear regulators to close in H2 2026 — an EU second-request/antitrust drag is the key slip risk and would defer the whole spin. Interim leverage sits above the 4.5x 2028 target, leaving refi/rate sensitivity. No cash-burn or covenant cliff flagged; the risk is timeline slip and discount persistence, not insolvency. Stock can drift toward the ~$9.73 52-wk low if the close is delayed.
positioning_washout: Not a dividend/yield washout — LBTY returns capital via buyback, not yield, so there is no mechanical income-holder exodus. The relevant flow is the OTHER direction: a 2027 Euronext listing of Ziggo Group creates fresh index-inclusion buying and a clean local investor base, while the simplified residual LBTY draws holdco/SoP specialists — a positive forced-flow event, not a liquidation.
moderator_conclusion: Verified, board-approved, precedent-backed SoP unlock with genuine 2.39 R:R asymmetry and a real (non-zero) floor — but it is a STAGING setup, not a fire-now ARB. The catalyst is PENDING_HARD: structure/venue/mechanism are named and the sponsor has executed the identical Sunrise playbook, but the spin itself is a 2027 window (soft date, ~378d horizon) gated on an H2-2026 regulatory close. That far, condition-precedent timeline is exactly the WATCH-FOR-CAPITULATION profile: the right move is to stage in and add on the Vodafone-close confirmation and when 2027 gets a hard listing/record date. Verdict B, conviction 4 — a strong, credible event one milestone short of imminent. value_conviction 4: the ~$9.50 floor against a $12 price and a ~$28 book gives a real margin of safety; capped from 5 only by the eroding-moat European-cable secular drift on the underlying assets, which is manageable (the listing/index unlock is largely independent of it).
interrogator_dossier:
# LBTYK - Liberty Global Ltd. | Catalyst Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

**Frame:** NOT a quality/compounding judgment. LBTYK is a European cable/telco holding company trading at a deep, structural holdco discount to the sum of its parts. The thesis is the SPREAD between the live price (~$11.6-12.0) and the value unlocked when the Benelux operating businesses are listed and distributed. The franchise looks cheap-and-bad (P/B ~0.41, levered, cord-cutting/overbuild exposure) BY DESIGN - the catalyst carries the thesis.

## 1. THE EVENT - Is it REAL, DATED, and BINDING?
VERIFIED. The board announced (8-K dated Feb 18, 2026) the formation of Ziggo Group - VodafoneZiggo (Netherlands) + Telenet (Belgium), ~13m customers, ~EUR6.6bn ($7.7bn) revenue - to be LISTED on Euronext Amsterdam in 2027, with 90% of the shares SPUN OFF to Liberty Global shareholders (subject to shareholder approval). Real, board-approved corporate action with named structure, named venue, named year.

Enabling step (hard regulatory gate): LBTY signed a definitive agreement to acquire Vodafone's 50% of the VodafoneZiggo JV for EUR1.0bn cash + a 10% stake in Ziggo Group. This buy-in is expected to close H2 2026, subject to regulatory approvals - it must clear BEFORE the spin proceeds, and is the first hard-dated milestone.

Sponsor credibility: LBTY executed the materially identical playbook with the Sunrise spin-off (completed late 2024) - separating the Swiss unit and distributing it to shareholders. Management (Fries/Mason) has a multi-decade record of value-unlock via splits, spins, and buybacks. The Sunrise precedent is the strongest credibility anchor: they have done exactly this, recently, successfully.

## 2. THE FLOOR - What backstops the price if the event fails?
Downside is bounded by the standalone equity value of the levered operating assets plus the residual holdco (Sunrise stub already separated, Telenet, VMO2 JV stake, ventures/Liberty Growth portfolio, net liquidity). At ~0.41x book and ~$4bn market cap on businesses targeting ~EUR500m adjusted FCF by 2028 with leverage falling to ~4.5x by 2028, the equity already prices a punitive holdco discount. The $9.50 floor corresponds to the 52-week low ($9.73) and a 'deal slips / no re-rate / discount persists' world. It is real because the cash flows and the aggressive buyback (continuous float shrink) provide ongoing per-share support absent the spin. The floor is NOT zero - no going-concern question; this is leverage + discount, not insolvency.

## 3. THE ASYMMETRY
- Live: ~$12.01 (current quote $11.60-11.64)
- Target (conservative event-resolved re-rate): $18.00
- Floor (deal slips / discount persists): $9.50
- Upside +$5.99 (~50%); downside -$2.51 (~21%); R:R ~2.39.
- Target is itself conservative: street/management SoP base case ~$27, book ~$28+ (P/B 0.41). $18 is a haircut-to-the-haircut, leaving headroom if the discount narrows even partway toward NAV - widening the asymmetry.

## 4. RED FLAGS / RISKS
- RED: Date soft on the SPIN itself - 2027 listing is a year ('as we get into the second half of next year'), not a fixed record/effective date; 378d horizon is far. WATCH/stage, not fire-now.
- RED: Regulatory contingency - the Vodafone buy-in (H2 2026 close) is a condition precedent; an EU second request / antitrust drag would slip the whole timeline.
- RED: Leverage - ~4.5x targeted by 2028 means today is higher; rate/refi risk on opco debt, though holdco ringfencing mitigates terminal risk.
- RED: Secular - European cable faces fiber overbuild / mobile substitution, compressing the multiple the spun entity earns and capping the re-rate, but NOT impairing the event (a separately-listed Ziggo with index inclusion is the unlock regardless).
- GREEN: Buyback - aggressive float shrink is a continuous per-share tailwind supporting the floor and compounding the eventual SoP-per-share.
- GREEN: Precedent - Sunrise (2024) proves management closes these.

## 5. VERDICT
The event is REAL and BOARD-APPROVED with a credible serial-spinner sponsor and a verified standalone floor. It is NOT yet hard-dated (2027 listing window, far horizon) and gated on an H2-2026 regulatory close - a PENDING / staging setup, not an imminent ARB. Asymmetry is genuine (2.39 R:R on a conservative target, more on the NAV-true target). Right posture: WATCH/stage; the catalyst strengthens as the Vodafone close lands and the 2027 listing gets a hard date.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: ERODING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: Catalyst is independently verified as real and board-approved, but it is NOT an arb spread with a hard floor. The spin is a 2027 LISTING WINDOW (no fixed record/effective date), gated on a regulatory close still pending (H2 2026, subject to approvals, unclosed as of Jun 2026). Critically, the '$9.50 floor' is a holdco-discount/SoP construct (~52-wk low $9.73), NOT a deal-break price or cash-recovery backstop. This is a voluntary self-restructuring, so there is no merger-break floor and no acquirer cash bid underneath the stock. If the European holdco discount simply persists (the chronic base rate for these names) or the regulatory close slips, nothing mechanically backstops the price. Asymmetry survives as a STAGING setup, not a fire-now closed spread.

catalyst.dated_milestone: Vodafone VodafoneZiggo JV buy-in (EUR1.0bn cash + 10% Ziggo stake) expected to close H2 2026, subject to regulatory approval; Ziggo Group Euronext Amsterdam listing + 90% spin-off targeted for 2027 (board 8-K Feb 18, 2026).
catalyst.binding_reason: Board-approved Ziggo Group (VodafoneZiggo + Telenet) Euronext Amsterdam listing with 90% spun to holders, sponsor-credible (identical Sunrise-2024 playbook executed), ~2.39:1 to an $18 target vs a real ~$9.50 floor against a ~$28 book. Skeptic CONFIRMED at cap 55: but the spin is a 2027 WINDOW (no fixed record/effective date), gated on an unclosed H2-2026 regulatory buy-in, and the floor is a 'discount-persists' SoP construct (no deal-break/cash backstop) on an ERODING-moat European-cable base. Stage in on the Vodafone close.
